locked_facts: Always produces virtual, erect, diminished image behind mirror
Image is ALWAYS between P and F behind mirror
Wider field of view than plane mirror
Used in rear-view mirrors and security mirrors
Focal length is positive in new Cartesian convention (focus behind mirror)
truth_statements: A convex mirror always forms a virtual, erect and diminished image
Convex mirrors are used as rear-view mirrors because they provide a wider field of view
The image formed by a convex mirror is always behind the mirror between the pole and focus
A convex mirror diverges light rays falling on it
Convex mirrors can form images of objects spread over a large area

aha_moment.what_to_show: Move the object from infinity to very close to the mirror. The image is ALWAYS virtual, erect, diminished, and stuck between P and F behind the mirror. Even at extreme positions, the nature never changes. This predictability is why convex mirrors are used in rear-view mirrors — the image is always upright and covers a wide field.
aha_moment.visual_emphasis: Show multiple object positions — the virtual image barely moves from the P-to-F region. Contrast this with a concave mirror where the image type changes dramatically with position.

simulation_states.label: Object far away — small virtual image near F
simulation_states.what_student_sees: Object far from mirror. Diverging reflected rays. Virtual image (dotted) near F behind mirror, very small.
simulation_states.what_to_emphasize: The convex mirror always diverges rays. The image is found by extending reflected rays backward.

simulation_states.label: Object closer — image moves toward P
simulation_states.what_student_sees: Object moved closer. Virtual image grows slightly, moves from F toward P.
simulation_states.what_to_emphasize: As the object approaches, the image slowly moves from F toward P but remains diminished.

simulation_states.label: Key insight — always virtual, erect, diminished
simulation_states.what_student_sees: All three standard rays shown with dotted virtual extensions behind mirror.
simulation_states.what_to_emphasize: No matter where you place the object, the image is always virtual, erect, diminished. This is the unique property of convex mirrors.

simulation_states.label: Interactive — move the object
simulation_states.what_student_sees: Slider to adjust object distance. Virtual image updates in real time.
simulation_states.what_to_emphasize: Verify: the image can never become real, inverted, or magnified with a convex mirror.

trigger_keywords: special case mein convex se real image
convex mirror real bana sakta hai kya
common_misconceptions.student_belief: A convex mirror can form a real image in some special case
common_misconceptions.actual_physics: A convex mirror ALWAYS forms a virtual, erect, diminished image. Since it diverges all incident rays, the reflected rays never converge in front of the mirror. There is no special case — the image nature is fixed.
common_misconceptions.simulation_emphasis: Show ray diagrams at extreme object positions — the reflected rays always diverge. Only backward extensions (behind mirror) converge, proving the image is always virtual.
